sanskrit: तपाम्यहमहं वर्षं निगृह्णाम्युत्सृजामि च। अमृतं चैव मृत्युश्च सदसच्चाहमर्जुन।।
transliteration: tapāmy aham ahaṃ varṣaṃ nigṛhṇāmy utsṛjāmi ca | amṛtaṃ caiva mṛtyuś ca sad asac cāham arjuna ||
literal_meaning: I give heat; I withhold and send forth rain; I am immortality and death; being and non-being are in Me, O Arjuna.
contextual_meaning: The psyche is invited to hold the full dialectic of opposites—creation/destruction, pleasure/pain, life/death—within one trans-personal Self, dissolving the ego’s need to split experience into ‘good’ or ‘bad’.
psychological_state: Post-dissolution calm that follows the ego’s surrender to an overarching Self capable of containing contradictions without defense.
trigger_scenario: A cancer patient who has accepted both remission and recurrence as part of the same life-process stops asking ‘Why me?’ and experiences spacious peace.
anchor_text: Hold every opposite—heat and cold, life and death—as one seamless Self.
